title: Campsis × intermedia
taxonomy: Campsis × intermedia is a hybrid of the American beautyberry (Campsis radicans) and the Chinese trumpet creeper (Campsis grandiflora), both of which are deciduous, flowering vines in the Bignoniaceae family. The Bignoniaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet vine or catalpa family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large group of flowering plants that also includes mint, honeysuckle, and snapdragon. Lamiales is a clade within the asterid clade, which is a subgroup of the angiosperms (flowering plants). The angiosperms are a large group of seed-producing plants that are divided into two main clades: the monocots and the dicots.
uses: Campsis × intermedia, also known as the American Trumpet Vine, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens, making it a subject of interest for the development of new antimicrobial agents. The plant's roots and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin infections. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain. Further research is needed to fully explore the medicinal uses of Campsis × intermedia and its potential benefits for human health.

constituents: Campsis × intermedia contains active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Campsis × intermedia, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to have potential in protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The saponins present in Campsis × intermedia have been reported to have potential in regulating cardiovascular health.

intro: Campsis × intermedia, also known as the American Trumpet Vine or Trumpet Creeper, is a deciduous, woody, perennial climber native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Campsis × intermedia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
